Vector Databases

A vector database is a type of database that stores and retrieves data as high-dimensional vectors, which are mathematical representations of features or attributes.

How They Work

Vector databases use specialized indexing techniques such as HNSW (Hierarchical Navigable Small World graphs) and IVF (Inverted File Index) to enable fast approximate nearest neighbor search across millions of vectors.

Key Features

  • Similarity Search: Find vectors most similar to a query vector using distance metrics like cosine similarity, dot product, or Euclidean distance.
  • Hybrid Search: Combine dense and sparse vector search for better retrieval quality.
  • Filtering: Apply metadata filters alongside vector search to narrow results.
  • Scalability: Handle billions of vectors with low latency.

Popular Vector Databases

Qdrant is an open-source vector database written in Rust, known for its performance and native support for named vectors and sparse vectors. Pinecone is a managed cloud vector database. Weaviate is an open-source vector database that supports multiple modalities.

Use Cases

Vector databases power many AI applications including semantic search, recommendation systems, anomaly detection, and retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) systems for large language models.
